findings.title: Path traversal via unvalidated --id flag enables writes outside workspace
findings.description: The `--id` flag value is passed unvalidated through `dataDir(id)` (which does `path.join("data", id)`) on line 68 and 85. With `id = "../../escape"`, `path.join` normalizes to `"../escape"` (it strips `data/..` but leaves the remaining `..`). Then `path.join(workspaceDir, "../escape")` yields a path OUTSIDE workspaceDir. The function proceeds to write `INFO.md` (line 89), `SETUP.md` (line 92, unconditionally — no existence check), and `project.json` (via `ensureProject` on line 84) at this attacker-controlled location. Files have mostly-fixed template content with `id` and `targetRel` interpolated, so attacker control over file content is limited, but this primitive can: (a) overwrite existing `SETUP.md` files at arbitrary paths (no existsSync guard), (b) create `INFO.md`/`project.json` at arbitrary paths, (c) with `--force`, overwrite `INFO.md` too. The CLAUDE.md threat model explicitly calls this out as a project-specific pattern to flag: `JSON/file writes under dataDir(...) with caller-supplied path segments — any segment containing .. or absolute paths could escape the per-project mirror`. This is exploitable when deepsec is wired into CI/CD with `--id` derived from PR titles, branch names, or other untrusted input.
findings.recommendation: Validate `id` early in `registerProject` to reject any value containing path separators or `..` segments. A simple regex check works: `if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9._-]*$/.test(id)) throw new Error(...)`. Also consider centralizing this validation in `dataDir()` itself (in `packages/core/src/paths.ts`) so every entry point is protected (the same concern applies to `--project-id` flags reaching `dataDir()` from `scan`, `metrics`, `export`, etc.).

revalidation.reasoning: The current code no longer passes opts.id directly into dataDir(id). registerProject derives id with validateProjectId(opts.id ?? path.basename(targetAbs)) before the first path construction, and validateProjectId only allows /^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._-]{0,63}$/. That rejects ../escape, absolute paths, slashes, backslashes, whitespace, and shell/path metacharacters before projectDataDir, ensureProject, INFO.md, or SETUP.md writes occur. In addition, @deepsec/core dataDir(projectId) now calls assertSafeSegment, which independently rejects empty values, '.', '..', absolute paths, null bytes, and both Unix and Windows path separators. The tests in packages/core/src/__tests__/paths.test.ts explicitly cover dataDir('..'), dataDir('../escape'), absolute project ids, and null bytes. I could not inspect commit history because this sandbox has no .git metadata, but the vulnerable flow described in the finding is patched in the current source.
